description: SAP qualification spec for C&A Technology. Identifies large US production-oriented companies (manufacturing, processing, extraction, generation) with $500M+ revenue that likely need SAP services.

waterfall_filter.description: Quick pre-disqualification before expensive LLM analysis - filters out competitors, wrong TLDs, and obvious non-fits

questions.prompt: What type of business is this? Look for: SAP consulting, IT services, systems integrator mentions (these are competitors). Also check if it appears to be a US-based company or has US operations.

disqualify_rules.log_message: SAP consulting, IT services, or systems integrator - competitor

output_schema.evidence: brief description of what was found

waterfall_filter.closing_instruction: Be lenient on the first pass. Only mark as IT_SERVICES_COMPETITOR if they clearly offer SAP consulting, IT services, or systems integration. Pass through everything else for deeper analysis.

categories.name: FOOD_AND_BEVERAGE
categories.description: Companies that manufacture food or beverage products (dairy, meat processing, bakery, confectionery, beverages, snacks, frozen foods, packaged foods)

categories.name: CPG
categories.description: Companies that manufacture consumer packaged goods excluding F&B (personal care, household products, cosmetics, beauty, pet care, baby products, consumer health, cleaning products)

categories.name: OIL_AND_GAS
categories.description: Companies that extract, refine, or process oil, gas, or petroleum products (oil extraction, gas production, refining, petrochemicals, drilling, pipeline operators)

categories.name: UTILITIES
categories.description: Companies that generate or distribute electricity, natural gas, or water (electric utilities, power generation, natural gas distribution, water utilities, renewable energy producers)

categories.name: MANUFACTURING
categories.description: Companies that manufacture physical products not covered by above categories (industrial equipment, machinery, automotive, aerospace, chemicals, metals, plastics, building products, electronics, medical devices)

categories.name: DISQUALIFIED
categories.description: Does not fit target profile - competitor, not production-oriented, non-US, or wrong industry

what_they_make.description: Products they manufacture, process, or produce - main product categories

size_indicators.description: Size indicators: employees, revenue, number of facilities, global presence

questions.question: What does this company do? Summarize their primary business in 1-2 sentences.
questions.guidance: Focus on their core business activity - what they make, process, or provide.

questions.question: What industry is this company in?
questions.guidance: Choose the most specific matching industry. Manufacturing is the catch-all for industrial/physical product makers. IT Services includes: SAP/ERP consulting, systems integrators, software consulting, managed IT services, SaaS platforms, digital transformation consulting. A company that USES software is NOT in IT Services - only companies whose PRIMARY BUSINESS is providing IT/software services.

questions.question: Is this company an INDUSTRIAL-SCALE MANUFACTURER? Do they own factories/plants that produce goods at scale?
questions.guidance: BE STRICT. YES ONLY if they: own manufacturing plants/factories that produce goods at industrial scale, operate refineries or processing facilities, extract oil/gas/minerals, generate electricity at power plants. NO if they: sell/distribute/resell products made by others, rebuild/refurbish/repair equipment (rebuilding is NOT manufacturing), provide services even if they sell some devices, are small custom shops (woodworking, artisan, bespoke furniture), buy and sell used equipment, provide monitoring/alert services with branded devices they don't manufacture, are primarily service companies with incidental product sales, grow plants/crops (agriculture, farming, nurseries, greenhouses are NOT manufacturing). The key test: Do they OWN FACTORIES that PRODUCE goods from raw materials at INDUSTRIAL SCALE? If not clearly yes, answer NO.

questions.question: Is this company US-based or do they have significant US operations?
questions.guidance: YES if: US headquarters, US subsidiary mentioned, US locations or operations referenced. NO if: headquarters outside US with no US subsidiary mentioned, website clearly for non-US market only.

questions.question: What products do they manufacture, process, or produce? List the main product categories.

Is this company likely to have $500M+ in annual revenue? Look for indicators: employee count (1000+ suggests large), multiple facilities/plants, global operations, Fortune 500/1000 mentions, publicly traded, or explicit revenue figures.
questions.guidance: Answer YES, NO, or UNCLEAR with reasoning. YES if strong indicators of large enterprise (1000+ employees, multiple manufacturing sites, global presence, public company). NO if clearly small (under 100 employees, single location, local business). UNCLEAR if insufficient evidence.

questions.question: Is SAP, ERP, or enterprise software mentioned on their website?

classification_logic.description: Classification based on industry, production orientation, and US presence. IT Services industry = competitor.

sufficiency_check.prompt_addition: Focus on understanding what this company does - do they manufacture, process, extract, or generate physical products? Look for About Us, Products, Services, Industries pages.

page_priority.description: Prioritize pages that reveal business model and products

qualified.reasoning: Major US meat processor and food manufacturer
US-based manufacturer of cereals, snacks, packaged foods
Global manufacturer of door/security hardware with US operations
Cosmetics/beauty manufacturer, US subsidiary of Japanese parent
US electric power generation and distribution

disqualified.reasoning: IT Services industry - systems integrator / consulting
IT Services industry - ERP software vendor
Equipment rebuilder/parts distributor - rebuilding is NOT manufacturing
Sells/monitors medical alert devices but doesn't manufacture them - service company
Buys and sells used warehouse equipment - reseller, not manufacturer
Small custom woodworking shop - artisan/bespoke, not industrial-scale manufacturing
Retail jeweler - NOT a competitor just because they use SAP. They sell jewelry, they don't offer SAP consulting.

edge_cases.handling: QUALIFIED - classify by primary manufacturing activity

edge_cases.scenario: US subsidiary of foreign parent (e.g., Shiseido USA)
Vertically integrated retailer with own food production
